mnemonics: Hadoop: Huge Amounts of Data On Parts. It's a framework for storing and processing huge datasets across parts (nodes) of a cluster.
HDFS: Hadoop's Distributed File System. Remember it as 'Hard Drives Forming Storage' to recall it's for distributed storage.
MapReduce: 'Massive Processing, Reduced Effort.' It maps data across nodes and then reduces the results into a single output.
keyPoints: Hadoop is an open-source framework for distributed storage and processing of large data sets across clusters of computers.
Its core components are HDFS (Hadoop Distributed File System) for storage and MapReduce for processing.
It is designed to scale up from a single server to thousands of machines, each offering local computation and storage.
commonMistakes: Thinking Hadoop is a database (it's a framework for data processing).
Believing MapReduce is Hadoop's only processing engine (YARN allows other engines like Spark).
Assuming data is stored in a single, large repository (it is distributed across many nodes).
